summary: CONSTITUTIONAL CHECKS: ALL 4 PASS — Cantonal satisfied (no new tables created; lcs already has lcs_canonical+lcs_errors), Registry-First (no CREATE TABLE so no CTB registration needed), Sovereign ID (no minting, only transitions existing MIDs), No sideways calls (all reads/writes within lcs schema: mid_ledger, lcs_canonical, lcs_errors). ENVELOPE: PASS — all 4 fields match (change_type=feature, architectural_flag=false, doctrine_version=2.8.0). SCOPE: PASS — 2 modified paths within migrations/ and tests/. No protected assets touched. EXECUTION: PASS — all 6 acceptance criteria met. (1) fn_mark_mid_ready(uuid) created with full READY gate: FOR UPDATE lock, validates MID exists, validates COMPILED state, validates canonical lifecycle (QUALIFIED/ENGAGED/CONVERTED permitted; SUSPECT/IDENTIFIED/SUPPRESSED blocked), calls fn_mid_suppression_check, updates dispatch_state=READY and ready_at=NOW(). (2) Five deterministic error paths all INSERT INTO lcs_errors then RETURN cleanly: MID not found (VALIDATION), wrong state (STATE_VIOLATION), no canonical record (VALIDATION), lifecycle block (LIFECYCLE_BLOCK), suppression block (SUPPRESSION). No RAISE-after-DML anywhere in function. (3) fn_dispatch_state_forward_only replaced with explicit whitelist FSM: MINTED→COMPILED→READY→SENT→{DELIVERED,FAILED,BOUNCED}. Terminal states blocked from further transition. All other transitions raise exception. QUEUED/DISPATCHED deprecated. (4) fn_mid_suppression_check(uuid) stub created, returns BOOLEAN TRUE always. Phase 3B placeholder. (5) REVOKE EXECUTE from PUBLIC, GRANT EXECUTE to lcs_worker on both fn_mark_mid_ready and fn_mid_suppression_check. Adapter mid_ledger protection inherited from Phase 2F REVOKE ALL. (6) 14 tests across 3 categories: FSM enforcement (7 — all allowed transitions + skip/backward/terminal-rewind blocks), READY gate (5 — success + 4 error paths), permissions (2 — lcs_worker EXECUTE on both functions). OBSERVATIONS: (1) Enum additions (COMPILED, READY, SENT) correctly placed outside BEGIN/COMMIT block with IF NOT EXISTS — PostgreSQL requires this for ALTER TYPE ADD VALUE. (2) Deprecated QUEUED/DISPATCHED given compatible ordinals in canonical monotonic guard (QUEUED=2 same as COMPILED, DISPATCHED=4 same as SENT) — backward compatible. (3) SECURITY INVOKER gap consistent with Phase 2F — fn_mark_mid_ready writes to mid_ledger/lcs_errors but lcs_worker has REVOKE ALL on those tables. Works as owner. SECURITY DEFINER needed for full least-privilege deployment. Non-blocking. (4) Suppression-block error path in fn_mark_mid_ready untestable because stub always returns TRUE — Phase 3B should add this test when stub is replaced.
